sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Elevated and fluctuating blood pressures.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports that his blood pressure has been elevated and fluctuating over the past three to four weeks, occasionally reaching over 180. He has experienced lightheadedness and slight headaches, particularly when the blood pressure is higher. He denies chest pain or difficulty breathing. He has been taking lisinopril 20mg twice daily and atorvastatin. He has been using a pillbox and an app to manage his medications, but found the app confusing. He reports feeling unsteady at times when experiencing lightheadedness. He has been taking tylenol for mild osteoarthritis pain and limiting his activity when the weather is cold.
Findings
Physical exam reveals a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, trace lower extremity edema, and mild bibasilar rales. Blood pressure readings have been inconsistent, ranging from 160/90 to 190/110. The patient reports feeling lightheaded and experiencing slight headaches when his blood pressure is elevated.
Assessment
The patient's blood pressure is out of control, requiring adjustments to his medication regimen. He has mild osteoarthritis and hyperlipidemia.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Increase lisinopril to 60mg twice daily and add hydrochlorothiazide 25mg daily. Order a cholesterol panel, CBC with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, and urinalysis. Continue atorvastatin. Follow up in four weeks to assess blood pressure control. Advise the patient to monitor his blood pressure twice daily and report any significant changes. Encourage him to stay hydrated and continue with his current activity level, but avoid overexertion. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks.
